modes_checked.mode_1_pipeline_health: deferred — directive-002 preempts; spot check showed no stranded patches >24h, agents producing per their cadences
modes_checked.mode_2_infrastructure: deferred — directive-002 preempts; but findings overlap with FND-01 below

findings.summary: FUSE-side operator-directives directory is stale relative to git, causing tinker Mode 0 dispatcher to mis-classify completed directives as pending. Third tinker run today (after tinker-2026-05-31T05-23 and tinker-2026-05-31T05-42) to read DIRECTIVE-001 as pending from FUSE despite git showing status='completed'/'completed_at'='2026-05-31T05:35:00Z'/'prop_id_authored'='PROP-065'.
findings.evidence: FUSE hash of DIRECTIVE-20260531-001-jsonl-destructive-overwrite.json: 74674ef069e6ebccf68385395402a34a (status='pending'). Git/clone hash: bd60d7f8300784b0930ce535ad96fefa (status='completed'). Tinker dispatcher reads from FUSE per the prompt design; without external check against git, would have re-authored PROP-065. This run side-stepped the false-positive by clone-checking before doing substantive work and routed instead to DIRECTIVE-002 (the next valid pending directive on git). Cause: PROP-064 Step 4c silent-skip (the same bug PROP-066 is designed to fix). Last sync-workspace-runs sentinel: 2026-05-30T01:16:37Z, ~28h stale. Tinker repaired the FUSE-side directive files (001 + 002) via clone copy as a one-time recovery; PROP-066 deployment will close the recurrence.
files_affected: monitor/tinker/operator-directives/DIRECTIVE-20260531-002-prop-064-deeper-silent-skip.json (FUSE refreshed in this run + marked completed by PROP-066 author)
findings.recommended_action: PROP-066 deployment closes the root cause. Until then, tinker's Mode 0 directive-discovery should cross-check FUSE-vs-git for any pending directive whose status differs from git. Adding this check as a tinker.md inline step is a candidate for a future PROP if PROP-066 doesn't fully resolve the staleness within 24h.
findings.status: PROP-066 authored; awaiting operator review + implementation

findings.summary: PROP-030 backlog metrics computed this run — all tiers green. open_issues_total=65, net_velocity_7d=+69 (134 closed - 65 created), assigned_analyst=43, age_ge_30d=0. No threshold fired.
findings.evidence: monitor/tinker/queue-history.jsonl row appended at 2026-05-31T05:58:58Z. Backlog has cleared substantially: was 230+ in early May (per CLAUDE.md PROP-030 context), now 65. Decider/curmudgeon BAU cadences are draining faster than analyst inflow. No action needed.
findings.recommended_action: Continue current cadences. Re-check on next tinker run.

findings.summary: Three tinker runs in 33 minutes (05-23, 05-42, 05-56) all hit FUSE-staleness on operator-directives. This run is the third instance — pattern is now well-evidenced (4+ events in ~13h counting tinker-2026-05-31T02-40 which separately found 5 stale FUSE files).
findings.evidence: tinker-2026-05-31T05-23 authored PROP-065 in response to DIRECTIVE-001. tinker-2026-05-31T05-42 attempted to author a duplicate PROP-065, wrote a stub redirect (PROP-065-jsonl-destructive-overwrite-fix.json with do_not_apply:true) when it discovered the duplicate via clone fetch. This run (05-56) detected the staleness pre-substantive-work and side-stepped, routing instead to DIRECTIVE-002. The pattern of 'tinker schedules cluster after operator activity, FUSE hasn't sync'd, duplicate work attempted' is recurring.
findings.recommended_action: PROP-066 deployment is the structural fix. As a secondary measure post-PROP-066: consider modifying tinker.md Mode 0 directive-discovery to fetch directly from origin/main rather than FUSE for the directive-status check. Defer until PROP-066 effectiveness is measured.
findings.status: PROP-066 covers; revisit if staleness persists post-deployment

PROP-065.verification: Confirmed implemented at 2026-05-31T05:52:49Z by operator-cowork-session. Verification per PROP-065's own verification_pattern: (1) workspace-sync.md contains GIT_APPEND_ONLY=( + is_git_append_only — VERIFIED (line 235); (2) build.js OWNERSHIP — not checked this run (operator-confirmed in implementation_notes); (3) CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules — not checked this run (operator-confirmed); (4) synthetic test — not run; (5) 36h baseline period — too soon (deployment was 04h ago).
PROP-065.verdict: DEPLOYED. Effectiveness measurement at next tinker Mode 3 run (post 2026-06-01T05:52Z target).

PROP-064.verification: STILL_BROKEN. Despite PROP-064's workstream A+B being present in workspace-sync.md (verified PRE_PULL_SHA capture at line 149, four-way classification at line 1210), Step 4c continues silent-skipping in production. Evidence: ONE sync-workspace-runs sentinel (bootstrap only) since deployment, ONE Step 4c commit message (`ea413b4` at 2026-05-30T17:11Z with improvised action), ZERO further Step 4c artifacts across ~18 subsequent cycles. This is the FND-01 of tinker-2026-05-31T02-40 + this run's diagnostic confirmation.
PROP-064.verdict: STILL_BROKEN — PROP-066 authored to close via helper-script extraction (resolves bash-variable-loss + LLM-skip-by-omission + silent-early-return).

PROP-061.verification: Architecturally sound; PROP-064 + PROP-066 amend its Step 4c implementation. Universal-pusher FUSE→git direction continues to work correctly (multiple successful FUSE→git rescues visible in workspace-sync run reports).
PROP-061.verdict: PARTIAL — git→FUSE direction depends on PROP-066 for reliable firing.
